heading: Figure 1
content:
Overall, the figure presents a comparison between OP-RAG and long-context models.

**(a)** The bar chart compares F1 scores of different models. OP-RAG models (blue) show higher F1 scores compared to long-context models (orange), with OP-RAG-48K achieving the highest score.

**(b)** The bar chart shows the average input token count for each model. Long-context models have a significantly higher token count compared to OP-RAG models, with Gemini-1.5-Pro having the highest.

sub_plots.a: The bar chart compares F1 scores of different models. OP-RAG models (blue) show higher F1 scores compared to long-context models (orange), with OP-RAG-48K achieving the highest score.
sub_plots.b: The bar chart shows the average input token count for each model. Long-context models have a significantly higher token count compared to OP-RAG models, with Gemini-1.5-Pro having the highest.

heading: Figure 2
content: A diagram comparing Vanilla RAG with the proposed order-preserve RAG. It shows a long document divided into 13 chunks, each with a similarity score. Vanilla RAG orders these chunks in descending similarity score, while order-preserve RAG maintains the original document order among the highest-scored chunks.

heading: Figure 3: The influence of context length on the performance of RAG. The evaluations are conducted on En.QA and EN.MC datasets of ∞Bench.
content:
This figure consists of two sub-plots illustrating the influence of context length on performance.

**(a)** The left plot shows the F1 score in relation to context length for EN.QA. Llama3.1-8B shows a peak at shorter context lengths and then declines, while Llama3.1-70B maintains a higher, more stable F1 score across the range.
**(b)** The right plot displays accuracy for EN.MC with context length. Llama3.1-8B shows a peak at a shorter context length, while Llama3.1-70B maintains higher accuracy throughout the context length spectrum.

sub_plots.b: The right plot displays accuracy for EN.MC with context length. Llama3.1-8B shows a peak at a shorter context length, while Llama3.1-70B maintains higher accuracy throughout the context length spectrum.

heading: Table 1: Comparisons among the long-context LLM without RAG, SELF-ROUTE mechanism (Li et al., 2024) and the proposed order-preserve (OP) RAG.
Figure 4: Comparisons between the proposed order-preserve RAG and vanilla RAG. The evaluations are conducted on En.QA and EN.MC datasets of ∞Bench, using Llama3.1-70B model.
content:
This figure consists of two sub-plots comparing order-preserve RAG and vanilla RAG.

**(a)** The plot shows the F1 score versus the number of retrieved chunks for the EN.QA dataset. The order-preserve RAG consistently outperforms vanilla RAG across all chunks, with F1 scores ranging from about 30 to 47.5.
**(b)** The plot depicts accuracy versus the number of retrieved chunks for the EN.MC dataset. Order-preserve RAG demonstrates higher accuracy compared to vanilla RAG, with scores stabilizing after 100 retrieved chunks.

sub_plots.a: The plot shows the F1 score versus the number of retrieved chunks for the EN.QA dataset. The order-preserve RAG consistently outperforms vanilla RAG across all chunks, with F1 scores ranging from about 30 to 47.5.
sub_plots.b: The plot depicts accuracy versus the number of retrieved chunks for the EN.MC dataset. Order-preserve RAG demonstrates higher accuracy compared to vanilla RAG, with scores stabilizing after 100 retrieved chunks.
